event_name: Native Jazz Quartet at the FireHouse Arts and Events Center
description: THE NATIVE JAZZ QUARTET is a hard-swinging and high energy group based on the swing/bebop tradition using indigenous and folk melodies. The NJQ was co-founded by Ed Littlefield (Lingít - drums) and Christian Fabian in 2011. Featured in the band is longtime member Reuel Lubag on piano, Michael Glynn on bass and Delbert Anderson (Diné) on Trumpet. Everyone in the band also contributes arrangements of melodies that come from their distinct heritage. The goal of the trio is to grow the Native Jazz movement and to attract a national and international audience of all ages. The Native Jazz Quartet is an exciting band, with an exciting message, that turns performances into cultural stories.

description: Viva Santana delivers the soaring guitar & electric Latin rhythms of Carlos Santana, led by Amie Said —authentic, soulful, and unforgettable APRIL 18 | SOOKE COMMUNITY HALL Doors: 7:00 PM Music: 8:00 PM Tickets: cascadiaconcerts.com or in person at Café Vosino & Forbes Pharmacy Full Bar Service | Kitchen Service Viva Santana is a high-energy tribute celebrating the soaring guitar and electric rhythms of Carlos Santana—music that demands deep feel, precision, and soul. Covering Santana isn’t something just anyone can do. It takes a special combination of tone, touch, and total commitment. Fronted by Amine Said , Viva Santana delivers exactly that. Amine has been immersed in the music of Santana since childhood, absorbing the phrasing, sustain, and emotional intensity that define Santana’s unmistakable sound. His playing captures not only the iconic tone, but the spiritual and rhythmic essence that makes this music timeless. Amine is also the founder of Coco Jafro , a respected Latin band known for its powerful grooves, rich percussion, and deep rhythmic authenticity. That background shines through in Viva Santana, where the rhythm section is just as vital as the guitar—driving, danceable, and relentlessly alive. With soaring solos, infectious Latin rock rhythms, and a deep respect for the original material, Viva Santana delivers a passionate, authentic tribute to one of rock’s greatest guitarists. This is not an imitation—it’s a celebration. A show for listeners, dancers, and anyone who believes that music is, indeed, the healing force. Featuring: Amine Said, Aidan Miller, Ray Azecedo, Linaldo Sans, Santiago Bigelow
location: 6645 East Sooke, Sooke, BC V9Z 1A5, CA

description: Storytelling & sacred song honouring Hanuman. Live music by Northcote + communal chanting. Sliding scale, all welcome. Join us Tuesday, March 31st for Hanuman Jayanti: Story & Sacred Song , a two-hour community gathering honouring the birth of Hanuman—the beloved deity revered for his strength, wisdom, humility, and unwavering devotion in the epic Ramayana . This special evening weaves together contemporary music, traditional mantra, storytelling, and shared reflection in a warm and welcoming space. Through chant and song, we celebrate Hanuman’s spirit of service and the power of devotion as a living, embodied practice. As part of the celebration, we will honour the traditional practice of chanting the Hanuman Chalisa eleven times , a devotional offering commonly observed during Hanuman Jayanti in India. This practice is also rooted in the spiritual lineage of the great Indian saint Neem Karoli Baba , whose temple continues to gather devotees for prayer, service, and remembrance. Throughout the evening, presenters will share stories from the Ramayana along with reflections from their own spiritual journeys, including their connection to Hanuman and the teachings of Neem Karoli Baba and Ram Dass. The gathering will also include an English recitation of the Hanuman Chalisa by Sean Sullivan , offering an opportunity to experience the meaning of this sacred text alongside the chanting. Whether you are new to devotional chanting or already familiar with mantra practice, this evening offers a welcoming space to sing, listen, learn, and connect in community. No prior experience with chanting or yoga philosophy is required. All are welcome. What to Bring Comfortable clothing, a water bottle, and anything that helps you feel at ease. You are also welcome to bring flowers, fruit, or photographs of teachers and loved ones to place on a shared puja table that we will create together. If you’d like to reflect during the evening, feel free to bring a journal. Please arrive a few minutes early to settle in. Hosted By Matt Goud – Musician & Mental Health Worker Frontman of Northcote and student of bhakti teachings. English recitations of the Hanuman Chalisa by Sean Sullivan Instagram: @northcotemusic
location: Ashtanga Yoga Victoria, 626A Fisgard St, Victoria, BC, CA

event_name: Living Opera: A free event for kids, families, and educators
description: Dive into the world of new opera creation with teaching artists Geoff and Nadia! In this lively, hands-on workshop, students will explore how music communicates emotion and brings stories to life! Using active listening and collaboration, students will identify the emotions they hear in opera excerpts, and the types of musical language used to convey them. Featuring live opera singing, students will play an interactive game that explores how composers express feelings through sound, and how that adds depth to the story! No musical experience required, just bring your imagination!
location: 925 Balmoral, VICTORIA, BC V8T 1A7, CA
